subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] mm: zswap: Tie per-CPU acomp_ctx lifetime to the pool.

reviews.author: Kanchana Sridhar
reviews.summary: Reviewer expressed gratitude and appreciation for simplifying code related to per-CPU caching, acknowledging their own past struggles with similar issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Good to know, Nhat! And thanks for the Acked-by :)

Best regards,
Kanchana

2026-04-13.patch_summary: This patch ties the lifetime of per-CPU accompaniment contexts (acomp_ctx) to their corresponding pool, ensuring they are properly cleaned up when a CPU is offlined or goes away. This simplifies handling CPU offlining for per-cpu structures and prevents potential memory leaks. The approach taken involves synchronizing the acomp_ctx lifetime with its associated pool.
